title: magnolia virginiana subsp. oviedoae
intro: Magnolia virginiana subsp. oviedoae, also known as the West Indian bay magnolia, is a subspecies of the evergreen tree Magnolia virginiana, native to the Caribbean and Central America. The bark, leaves, and roots of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with medicinal properties including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever and relieving pain, as well as treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Magnolia virginiana subsp. oviedoae include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
